codeReview.summary: Implementation covers most install-validation requirements (GitHub URL checks, pluginPath normalization, zip size/traversal checks, manifest validation, dependency install + rollback) with strong tests, but one blocking security gap remains in the repo install extraction path.

issues.description: install_from_repo() extracts downloaded archives via zf.extractall(tmp_dir / 'repo') instead of using the guarded _safe_extract() traversal checks used for zip uploads. This leaves repository installs without explicit traversal validation and keeps security behavior inconsistent with VAL-EXT-004 expectations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Baseline-validation guidance appears misaligned with mission-known frontend failures, leading to procedural deviation despite acceptable feature verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback marks followedProcedure=false because baseline command `uv run pytest -x -q --tb=short && bun run test --run` hit known pre-existing covalt-n8n Vitest failures; mission AGENTS.md documents these failures as pre-existing, and .factory/services.yaml provides `test_frontend_filtered` for this exact case.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton and commit diff for feature external-plugin-install-validation (worker session 98197b09-d51d-4f89-a8a0-339e7cd1d57c, commit 2545e86e848e3196e1f8da15fe8ce2c88b77e033). The feature is close to complete, but it is not production-ready due to a blocking repo-archive extraction security gap.
